chunk_summary: This section outlines age-banded calcium and phosphate SDI targets for pediatric CKD, emphasizing proper mineralization, avoiding complications, and stage-based lab monitoring frequencies for CKD-MBD management.
learning_objectives: Understand age-specific calcium and phosphate SDI targets for pediatric CKD patients.
Learn how to adjust dietary and medication-based calcium and phosphate intake to prevent complications like vascular calcification or adynamic bone disease.
Identify appropriate CKD stage-based monitoring frequencies for calcium, phosphate, and PTH levels.
Recognize the importance of using 'height age' for children with short stature in dietary planning.
key_facts: Age-specific calcium SDI targets: 450–700 mg/day (1–3 years), 700–1000 mg/day (4–10 years), 900–1300 mg/day (11–17 years).
Age-specific phosphate SDI targets: 250–500 mg/day (1–3 years), 440–800 mg/day (4–10 years), 640–1250 mg/day (11–17 years).
Total calcium intake (diet + medications) should not exceed twice the SDI except under special circumstances with monitoring.
CKD G2–G3: monitor calcium/phosphate every 6–12 months; CKD G4: every 3–6 months; CKD G5/dialysis: every 1–3 months.
Persistent hyperphosphatemia or hyperparathyroidism is managed by dietary phosphate restriction and phosphate binders, not by removing core protein foods.
PTH monitoring is less frequent in early CKD stages but increases to every 3–6 months in advanced CKD or dialysis.
